products.title: Kenya Nyeri AA
products.body_html: A washed AA lot from smallholder farms around Nyeri, milled at the Othaya cooperative. Expect blackcurrant and grapefruit up front with a syrupy sweetness that holds as the cup cools. We roast it light to protect the acidity, and it shines as a pour-over, an AeroPress, or iced. Roasted to order and shipped within 24 hours.

products.title: Firebreak Espresso Blend
products.body_html: Our year-round espresso: a rotating base of Brazilian naturals for body and cocoa, lifted by a washed Central American component for a clean red-fruit finish. Built to cut through milk without turning bitter, and forgiving across a wide range of machines. Dialing-in notes ship with every bag, and our baristas answer recipe questions by email.

products.title: Harvest Calendar Subscription
products.body_html: Coffee that follows the harvest, not a warehouse. Each month we ship whatever just landed at its seasonal peak — Ethiopian naturals in winter, Kenyans in spring, Centrals through summer — with an origin card explaining who grew it and why we bought it. Skip a month, change grind, or cancel from a single link in every email. Always free shipping.

products.title: Colombia El Paraíso Geisha
products.body_html: A thermal-shock washed Geisha from Diego Bermúdez's farm in Cauca that drinks more like a tea than a coffee: jasmine, white peach, and a lychee-candy sweetness with almost no bitterness. This is a limited lot of roughly 400 bags per season, roasted only on Thursdays so it ships at absolute peak. If it's in stock, it's fresh.

products.title: Hearthline Ceramic Mug
products.body_html: A 10-ounce stoneware mug thrown for us by Ash Creek Pottery, glazed in the same rust orange as our roastery door. The walls are thick enough to keep a slow morning cup warm for the better part of an hour, the handle fits three fingers comfortably, and it is dishwasher and microwave safe. Each one varies slightly — that's the point.

products.title: Pour-Over Starter Set
products.body_html: Everything a first pour-over needs and nothing it doesn't: a plastic V60 (plastic holds temperature better than you'd think and never cracks), a pack of 100 filters, a 12-ounce bag of our Firebreak blend ground for filter, and a laminated recipe card with the exact pour schedule we teach in roastery classes. Add a scale later; start here now.
